meta.title: AI Exposure of the Canadian Job Market
meta.primary_rollup_note: The dashboard rolls canonical NOC 2021 unit groups through the 45 official major groups.
meta.metric_model_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables are published at a coarser occupation grain than official NOC 2021 unit groups, so the unit-group labour metrics are allocated using ESDC unit employment weights. Outlook remains direct from the ESDC unit-group release.
meta.exposure_metric_scale: 0-10 display score derived from the share of workers in high-exposure EPIAC occupations
meta.exposure_note: Official EPIAC fields are mapped from published StatCan occupation groups onto the canonical NOC 2021 unit groups.
meta.profile_layer_note: Official OaSIS occupational profiles attach to canonical NOC 2021 unit groups through an explicit generated mapping table.
meta.source: Statistics Canada annual occupation tables rebuilt onto the canonical NOC 2021 spine

source_tables.name: 14-10-0416-01 Labour force characteristics by occupation, annual

oasis.title: Occupational and Skills Information System (OaSIS) - 2025 Version 1.0

family.title: Sales and service support occupations
family.definition: This major group comprises sales and service support occupations. These occupations come with Training, Education, Experience and Responsibility (TEER) identified as TEER 5. Occupations classified in TEER 5, as those included in this group with a 5 as the second digit of their code, are typically accessible with short work demonstration and no formal educational requirements.
family.exposure_rationale: Aggregated from canonical unit groups. EPIAC values roll up unit-level mappings from published StatCan occupation groups.
family.metric_rationale: This major group aggregates canonical unit groups. Labour-market counts and wages reflect the unit-group estimates allocated from published StatCan annual occupation tables using ESDC employment weights.
family.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Support occupations in sales and service (66, 67)

units.title: Cashiers
units.minor_group_title: Cashiers and other sales support occupations
units.definition: Cashiers operate cash registers, optical price scanners, computers or other equipment to record and accept payment for the purchase of goods, services and admissions. They are employed in stores, restaurants, theatres, recreational and sports establishments, currency exchange booths, government offices, business offices and other service, retail and wholesale establishments.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Support occupations in sales and service (66, 67).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Support occupations in sales and service (66, 67)

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Cashiers operate cash registers, optical price scanners, computers or other equipment to record and accept payment for the purchase of goods, services and admissions.
employment_requirements: Some secondary school education is usually required. High school diploma may be required by some employers.
A casino gaming licence may be required for cashiers working in gambling casinos.
additional_information: Cashiers may progress to supervisory positions, such as head cashier, with additional training or experience.

core_competencies.statement: to quickly and accurately total purchases, calculate change and process discounts
to respond to the emotions and needs of customers for better interactions and service
to understand and respond to customer requests and concerns effectively
to accurately read and input product codes, scan barcodes and process transactions on registers and screens
to focus on relevant tasks amidst potential distractions, such as processing payments, verifying prices and managing customer interactions
to accurately process transactions to reduce errors and maintain a balanced register, including counting change, applying discounts and handling returns
to interact professionally with clients during transactions, handle complaints and ensure a positive client experience

units.title: Service station attendants
units.minor_group_title: Cashiers and other sales support occupations
units.definition: Service station attendants employed in automotive service stations sell fuel and other automotive products and perform such services as fuelling, cleaning, lubricating and performing minor repairs to motor vehicles. Those employed in marinas sell fuel, rent boats and related equipment, and maintain marina facilities.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Support occupations in sales and service (66, 67).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Support occupations in sales and service (66, 67)

oasis_profiles.profile_title: Automotive and watercraft service station attendants
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Service station attendants employed in automotive service stations sell fuel and other automotive products and perform such services as fuelling, cleaning, lubricating and performing minor repairs to motor vehicles. Those employed in marinas sell fuel, rent boats and related equipment, and maintain marina facilities.
employment_requirements: Propane and natural gas pump attendants may require an operator's licence.
Some secondary school education is usually required.

core_competencies.statement: to choose the appropriate tools and equipment for various repairs, minimizing damage and ensuring quality service
to identify and fix issues with vehicles or marina equipment, as well as performing routine maintenance to prevent future breakdowns
to lift heavy fuel hoses, equipment or automotive parts
to precisely handle small tools, parts and controls when performing repairs or maintenance tasks
to work with other staff members to ensure smooth operations, including customer service or repair and maintenance tasks
to interact with customers, answer questions and address specific concerns about service stations for a positive customer experience

units.title: Store shelf stockers, clerks and order fillers
units.minor_group_title: Cashiers and other sales support occupations
units.definition: Store shelf stockers, clerks and order fillers pack customers' purchases, price items, stock shelves with merchandise, and fill online, mail and telephone orders. They are employed in retail establishments, such as grocery, hardware and department stores, and in warehouses.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Support occupations in sales and service (66, 67).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Support occupations in sales and service (66, 67)

oasis_profiles.profile_title: Store shelf stockers, clerks and order fillers
oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Store shelf stockers, clerks and order fillers
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Store shelf stockers, clerks and order fillers pack customers' purchases, price items, stock shelves with merchandise, and fill online, mail and telephone orders.
employment_requirements: Some secondary school education is usually required.

core_competencies.statement: to anticipate customer requirements, provide appropriate assistance and contribute to a positive shopping experience
to organize and manage inventory efficiently, including restocking shelves and coordinating the flow of merchandise
to handle small items, label and pack orders
to lift and move heavy boxes and merchandise safely to stock shelves and handle large volumes of inventory
to ensure items are correctly priced and stocked in the right location and orders are filled accurately
to be familiar with the processes involved in the movement and management of goods, including inventory control and order fulfillment

units.title: Other sales related occupations
units.minor_group_title: Cashiers and other sales support occupations
units.definition: Workers in other sales related occupations sell goods or services during home demonstrations or by telephone soliciting, retail exhibitions or street vending. They are employed by a wide range of retail and wholesale establishments, manufacturers, telemarketing companies and call centres, or they may be self-employed.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Support occupations in sales and service (66, 67).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Support occupations in sales and service (66, 67)

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Demonstrators and direct distributors sell products and services by phone or in person at home demonstrations or trade shows.
main_duties: Contact potential customers by phone or through personal contacts

Arrange sales demonstration appointments, and show, describe and sell goods or services at wholesale, retail and industrial establishments, at exhibitions, trade shows or directly to individual customers or at sales parties and private homes
Deliver purchased goods to customers.
employment_requirements: Some secondary school education is usually required. A high school diploma may be required by some employers.
additional_information: Progression to related supervisory positions is possible with additional training or experience.

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Door-to-door salespersons and street vendors solicit individuals in their homes or on the street and sell goods and services.
main_duties: Solicit sales and sell merchandise to residents of private homes
Set up and display merchandise on sidewalks or at public events to sell to the public.
employment_requirements: Some secondary school education is usually required. A high school diploma may be required by some employers.
Self-employed street vendors and door-to-door salespersons may require a municipal merchant's permit.
additional_information: Progression to related supervisory positions is possible with additional training or experience.

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Telephone solicitors and telemarketers sell goods and services by telephone.
main_duties: Contact businesses or private individuals, by telephone, to solicit sales for goods or services.
employment_requirements: Some secondary school education is usually required. A high school diploma may be required by some employers.
additional_information: Progression to related supervisory positions is possible with additional training or experience.

units.title: Food and beverage servers
units.sub_major_group_title: Support occupations in food, accommodation and tourism
units.definition: Food and beverage servers take patrons' food and beverage orders and serve orders to patrons. They are employed in restaurants, hotels, bars, taverns, private clubs, banquet halls and similar establishments.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Support occupations in sales and service (66, 67).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Support occupations in sales and service (66, 67)

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Food and beverage servers take patrons' food and beverage orders and serve orders to patrons.
employment_requirements: An apprenticeship program for food and beverage servers is available, but voluntary, in Quebec and Saskatchewan.
Sommeliers may require courses in wine selection and service orexperience as a formal waiter/waitress.
Formal waiters/waitresses may require completion of a one- or two-year apprenticeship program orcollege or vocational school courses.
On-the-job training is usually provided.
Completion of secondary school may be required.
Responsible beverage service certification is usually required for employees serving alcoholic beverages.

example_titles.job_title_text: captain waiter/waitress - food and beverage services
assistant waiter/waitress - food and beverage services

core_competencies.statement: to recognize non-verbal cues and adjust their service approach accordingly to enhance the guest experience
to understand guests' requests, clarify any ambiguities and ensure orders are processed correctly
to navigate through crowded spaces, balance trays and manage multiple tasks simultaneously without bumping into guests or dropping items
to stand, walk and carry heavy trays or plates throughout extended periods

core_competencies.competency: Concern for Others and Social Orientation
core_competencies.statement: to create a positive dining experience, be attentive to guests’ needs, respond empathetically to complaints and ensure everyone feels welcomed and valued
to professionally interact with customers, handle complaints, offer solutions to problems and ensure food service experiences are positive

units.title: Food counter attendants, kitchen helpers and related support occupations
units.sub_major_group_title: Support occupations in food, accommodation and tourism
units.definition: Food counter attendants and food preparers prepare, heat and finish cooking simple food items and serve customers at food counters. Kitchen helpers, food service helpers and dishwashers clear tables, clean kitchen areas, wash dishes, and perform various other activities to assist workers who prepare or serve food and beverages. They are employed by restaurants, cafés, hotels, fast food outlets, cafeterias, hospitals and other establishments.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Support occupations in sales and service (66, 67).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Support occupations in sales and service (66, 67)

oasis_profiles.profile_title: Food counter attendants and food preparers
oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Food counter attendants, kitchen helpers and related support occupations
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Food counter attendants and food preparers prepare, heat and finish cooking simple food items and serve customers at food counters.
main_duties: Take customers' orders
Clean, peel, slice and trim foodstuffs using manual and electric appliances
Use deep fryer, grill, oven, dispensers and other equipment to prepare fast food items such as sandwiches, hamburgers, fries, salads, ice cream dishes, milkshakes and other beverages
Portion, assemble, and wrap food or place it directly on plates for service to patrons, and package take-out food
Use equipment to prepare hot beverages such as coffee and tea specialties
Serve customers at counters or buffet tables
Stock refrigerators and salad bars and keep records of the quantities of food used
May receive payment for food items purchased.
employment_requirements: On-the-job training is provided.
Some secondary school education is usually required.
additional_information: Movement into other occupations within food preparation and service, such as cook or waiter, is possible with further training and experience.
There is considerable mobility among jobs in this unit group.

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Food counter attendants, kitchen helpers and related support occupations
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Kitchen helpers perform various other activities to assist workers who prepare or serve food and beverages.
main_duties: Wash and peel vegetables and fruit
Wash work tables, cupboards and appliances
Remove trash and clean kitchen garbage containers
Unpack and store supplies in refrigerators, cupboards and other storage areas
Sweep and mop floors, and perform other duties to assist cook and kitchen staff.
employment_requirements: On-the-job training is provided.
Some secondary school education is usually required.
additional_information: Movement into other occupations within food preparation and service, such as cook or waiter, is possible with further training and experience.
There is considerable mobility among jobs in this unit group.

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Food counter attendants, kitchen helpers and related support occupations
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Food service helpers clear and clean tables and trays in eating establishments.
main_duties: Clear and clean tables and trays in eating establishments
Bring clean dishes, flatware and other items to serving areas and set tables
Replenish condiments and other supplies at tables and in serving areas
Remove dishes before and after courses
Perform other duties such as scraping and stacking dishes, carrying linen to and from laundry area and running errands.
employment_requirements: On-the-job training is provided.
Some secondary school education is usually required.
additional_information: Movement into other occupations within food preparation and service, such as cook or waiter, is possible with further training and experience.
There is considerable mobility among jobs in this unit group.

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Food counter attendants, kitchen helpers and related support occupations
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Dishwashers wash dishes, glassware, flatware, pots and pans using dishwasher or by hand.
main_duties: Wash dishes, glassware, flatware, pots and pans using dishwasher or by hand
Place dishes in storage area
Scour pots and pans, and may clean and polish silverware.
employment_requirements: On-the-job training is provided.
Some secondary school education is usually required.
additional_information: Movement into other occupations within food preparation and service, such as cook or waiter, is possible with further training and experience.
There is considerable mobility among jobs in this unit group.

units.title: Meat cutters and fishmongers - retail and wholesale
units.sub_major_group_title: Support occupations in food, accommodation and tourism
units.definition: Meat cutters and fishmongers prepare and package pieces of meat, poultry and fish that are sold by food retailers or wholesalers. They use tools to cut, trim and package meat, poultry and fish on a daily basis. They are employed in grocery stores, fish stores, or they may be self-employed.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Support occupations in sales and service (66, 67).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Support occupations in sales and service (66, 67)

oasis_profiles.profile_title: Meat cutters and fishmongers - retail and wholesale
oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Meat cutters and fishmongers - retail and wholesale
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Meat cutters and fishmongers prepare and package pieces of meat, poultry and fish that are sold by food retailers or wholesalers. They use tools to cut, trim and package meat, poultry and fish on a daily basis.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school may be required.
On-the-job training in food stores is usually offered to meat cutters and fishmongers for retail sale.
An apprenticeship program in meat cutting is available but voluntary.
Trade certification for meat cutter is available, but voluntary, in Saskatchewan and British Columbia.

example_titles.job_title_text: meat portion cutter - retail or wholesale

core_competencies.statement: to control and maintain equipment tools and machinery, such as saws, grinders and slicers
to handle the receipt, storage and rotation of meat, poultry or fish, ensuring compliance with health and safety standards
to safely lift and maneuver large cuts of fish or meat, boxes or other supplies
to safely use sharp tools in order to perform precise cuts and trims of fish or meat
to maintain high standards of quality and hygiene and ensure the final product meets customers' expectations

core_competencies.competency: Knowledge in Livestock, Farm animals & Wildlife
core_competencies.statement: to understand how livestock are raised for meat, poultry or fish products and apply sourcing practices that ensure products come from sustainable, well-managed, traceable and regulatory compliant operations

units.title: Support occupations in accommodation, travel and facilities set-up services
units.sub_major_group_title: Support occupations in food, accommodation and tourism
units.minor_group_title: Support occupations in accommodation, travel, tourism and amusement services
units.definition: Workers in support occupations in accommodation, travel and facilities set-up services escort guests to their rooms, carry travellers' luggage at airports, in railway stations and aboard ships, clean and maintain public areas and passengers' rooms aboard ships and trains, and set up rooms and associated furnishings, commercial displays, exhibits, equipment and booths in facilities and establishments. They are employed by hotels, conference centres, retail establishments, passenger transport companies and throughout the private and public sectors.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

epiac_source_groups: Support occupations in sales and service (66, 67)

oasis_profiles.profile_title: Guest service attendants and baggage porters
oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Support occupations in accommodation, travel and facilities set-up services
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Guest service attendants and baggage porters escort incoming guests to their rooms and carry travellers' luggage.
main_duties: Carry luggage for hotel guests, escort incoming guests to their rooms
Check rooms to ensure they are in order
Offer information regarding features of rooms, services of hotel and points of interest
Assist guests with special needs
Attend to group arrivals and departures
Receive and deliver items and messages for guests
Handle guest complaints and requests and maintain luggage storage areas
Convey travellers' luggage at railway stations and airports by hand or hand truck and arrange for ground transportation.
employment_requirements: Some secondary school education may be required.

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Support occupations in accommodation, travel and facilities set-up services
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Ship and train service attendants carry luggage for passengers aboard and maintain public areas and passengers' rooms aboard ships and trains.
main_duties: Set and clear tables, serve food and beverages, clean sleeping cars and cabins, make beds, wash dishes and maintain washroom supplies
Carry luggage for passengers aboard ships
Ensure customer satisfaction and comfort.
employment_requirements: Some secondary school education may be required.

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Support occupations in accommodation, travel and facilities set-up services
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Facilities workers set-up, install, reconfigure, relocate and dismantle temporary partitions and furnishings of rooms. They also assemble, rotate and disassemble window displays, showcases and signage according to floor plans or instructions.
main_duties: Set-up, install, reconfigure, relocate and dismantle temporary partitions and furnishings of rooms
Assemble, rotate and disassemble window displays, showcases and signage according to floor plans or instructions
Assist with installation of audiovisual equipment and help in resolving problems during use
May perform minor repairs on installations and routine maintenance of audiovisual equipment.
employment_requirements: Some secondary school education may be required.

units.title: Operators and attendants in amusement, recreation and sport
units.sub_major_group_title: Support occupations in food, accommodation and tourism
units.minor_group_title: Support occupations in accommodation, travel, tourism and amusement services
units.definition: Operators and attendants in amusement, recreation and sport assist patrons, collect tickets and fees and supervise the use of recreational and sports equipment. They are employed by amusement parks, fairs, exhibitions, carnivals, arenas, billiard parlours, bowling alleys, golf courses, ski centres, tennis clubs, campgrounds and other recreational and sports facilities.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

epiac_source_groups: Support occupations in sales and service (66, 67)

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Operators and attendants in amusement, recreation and sport
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Amusement attraction operators operate rides and other attractions and perform routine maintenance and safety inspections of attraction equipment.
main_duties: Drive trucks, vans and other vehicles to transport amusement rides, games and other attractions to amusement attraction sites
Set up rides, fun houses, game concessions and other amusement attractions
Perform routine maintenance and safety inspections of attraction equipment
Operate rides and other attractions, oversee game activities and ensure safety of participants
May sell tickets.
employment_requirements: On-the-job training is usually provided.
Some secondary school education may be required.

oasis_profiles.profile_title: Attendants in amusement, recreation and sport
oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Operators and attendants in amusement, recreation and sport
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Attendants in amusement, recreation and sport assist patrons, collect tickets and fees and supervise the use of recreational and sports equipment.
main_duties: Collect tickets and fees, and rent or sell sports and accessory equipment
Schedule the use of recreational facilities such as golf courses, tennis courts, bowling alleys, fitness clubs, campgrounds and other similar facilities
Operate recreational facility equipment such as ski lifts, ice rink equipment and snow making machines
Assist patrons on and off ski lifts and amusement park rides, secure and release safety belts and bars and monitor equipment to detect wear and damage
Clean and maintain recreational facilities and grounds.
employment_requirements: On-the-job training is usually provided.
Some secondary school education may be required.

units.title: Pet groomers and animal care workers
units.sub_major_group_title: Support occupations in food, accommodation and tourism
units.definition: Animal care workers feed, handle, train and groom animals and assist veterinarians, animal health technologists and technicians and animal breeders. Pet groomers clip coats, bathe and otherwise groom pets. They are employed by animal hospitals and clinics, animal shelters, breeding and boarding kennels, zoos, laboratories, retail pet shops, dog training schools, pet grooming establishments, or may be self-employed.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

epiac_source_groups: Support occupations in sales and service (66, 67)

oasis_profiles.profile_title: Pet groomers and animal care workers
oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Pet groomers and animal care workers
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Animal care workers feed, handle, train and groom animals and assist veterinarians, animal health technologists and technicians and animal breeders. Pet groomers clip coats, bathe and otherwise groom pets.
employment_requirements: Depending on the type of work performed, training courses in animal handling, grooming or dog training are usually required. Animal health or veterinary assistant college courses may be required for animal care workers.
Completion of secondary school is usually required.
additional_information: Progression to animal health technology occupations is possible with additional training.

example_titles.job_title_text: animal trainer (except horses or marine mammals)

core_competencies.statement: to oversee animals during minor procedures, grooming or day-to day to detect any signs of illness or distress
to understand instructions from veterinarians, animal health professionals and clients
to bend, reach and move animals around to groom or examine them
to hold or support animals in place safely without causing injury, such as during grooming, feeding or handling
to observe signs of health issues, accurately administer treatments and meticulously groom animals to maintain their health
to engage with pet owners effectively and ensure their needs or concerns are addressed professionally and empathetically

units.title: Other support occupations in personal services
units.sub_major_group_title: Support occupations in food, accommodation and tourism
units.definition: Workers in other support occupations in personal services provide psychic consulting, fortune-telling, astrological services and other personal services. They are employed by call centres and personal service establishments, or are self-employed.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. oasis_profiles.profile_title: Other support occupations in personal services
oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Other support occupations in personal services
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Workers in other support occupations in personal services provide psychic consulting, fortune-telling, astrological services and other personal services.
employment_requirements: A period of training under the guidance of a practitioner is usually required.

core_competencies.statement: to interpret clients' emotions, reactions and non-verbal cues to tailor interactions to better meet clients' needs
to manage client expectations, reinforce the credibility of the service provided and encourage clients to engage with the process
to develop advice, insight or guidance tailored to clients' specific situations
to respond to clients' questions, comments and concerns in a thoughtful manner
to adjust their approach based on the client's needs, mood and preferences, ensuring a personalized service
to build trust with clients, manage expectations and handle feedback effectively and professionally

units.title: Light duty cleaners
units.sub_major_group_title: Support occupations in cleaning and related services
units.definition: Light duty cleaners clean lobbies, hallways, offices and rooms of hotels, motels, resorts, hospitals, schools, office buildings and private residences. They are employed by hotels, motels, resorts, recreational facilities, hospitals and other institutions, building management companies, cleaning service companies and private individuals. Housekeepers perform housekeeping duties in residential, and institutional settings.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Light duty cleaners clean lobbies, hallways, offices and rooms of hotels, motels, resorts, hospitals, schools, office buildings and private residences.
main_duties: Sweep, mop, wash, wax and polish floors
Clean, disinfect and polish kitchen and bathroom fixtures and appliances
Clean and disinfect public areas such as changing rooms, showers and elevators
Disinfect operating rooms and other hospital areas
Wash windows, walls and ceilings.
May handle complaints.
employment_requirements: There are no specific education requirements for occupations in this unit group.
additional_information: Progression to supervisory cleaning positions is possible with additional training or experience.

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Housekeepers perform housekeeping duties in residential, and institutional settings.
main_duties: Dust furniture and vacuum carpeting and area rugs, draperies and upholstered furniture
Make beds, change sheets and distribute clean towels and toiletries
Attend to guests' requests for extra supplies
Stock linen closets and other supplies' areas
Report and store lost and found items
May provide basic information on facilities
employment_requirements: There are no specific education requirements for occupations in this unit group.
Certification for housekeeping room attendants or similar certification may be required by some employers.
additional_information: Progression to supervisory cleaning positions is possible with additional training or experience.

units.title: Specialized cleaners
units.sub_major_group_title: Support occupations in cleaning and related services
units.definition: Specialized cleaners clean and refurbish building exteriors, carpets, chimneys, industrial equipment, ventilation systems, windows and other surfaces, using specialized equipment and techniques. They are employed by specialized cleaning service companies or they may be self-employed.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Carpet and upholstery cleaners clean carpets and upholstery using specialized equipment and techniques.
main_duties: Operate cleaning machines to clean carpets, rugs and upholstered furniture on customers' premises or in carpet and upholstery cleaning establishments.
employment_requirements: There are no specific education requirements for occupations in this unit group.
On-the-job training is usually provided.
Previous experience in a related position may be required for some occupations in this unit group.
additional_information: Progression to supervisory positions is possible with additional training or experience.

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Chimney cleaners and sandblasters clean chimneys and building exteriors using hand tools and sandblasting, pressurized steam or other equipment.
main_duties: Clean soot and creosote from chimneys and fireplaces using hand tools and industrial vacuum cleaners
Clean building exteriors, tanks and industrial equipment using sandblasting, pressurized steam or hydroblasting equipment.
employment_requirements: There are no specific education requirements for occupations in this unit group.
On-the-job training is usually provided.
Previous experience in a related position may be required for some occupations in this unit group.
additional_information: Progression to supervisory positions is possible with additional training or experience.

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Furnace and ventilation system cleaners clean furnaces and ventilation systems using specialized equipment and techniques.
main_duties: Clean ducts, vents and filters of furnaces in residences and commercial buildings using hand tools and industrial vacuum cleaners.
employment_requirements: There are no specific education requirements for occupations in this unit group.
On-the-job training is usually provided.
Previous experience in a related position may be required for some occupations in this unit group.
additional_information: Progression to supervisory positions is possible with additional training or experience.

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Vehicle cleaners clean interiors and exteriors of vehicles using specialized equipment and techniques.
main_duties: Clean the interior and exterior of automobiles, buses, streetcars, railway cars and subway cars.
employment_requirements: There are no specific education requirements for occupations in this unit group.
On-the-job training is usually provided.
Previous experience in a related position may be required for some occupations in this unit group.
additional_information: Progression to supervisory positions is possible with additional training or experience.

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Window cleaners clean windows and other glass surfaces using specialized equipment and techniques.
main_duties: Wash and clean interior and exterior windows and other glass surfaces in low-rise and highrise buildings.
employment_requirements: There are no specific education requirements for occupations in this unit group.
On-the-job training is usually provided.
Previous experience in a related position may be required for some occupations in this unit group.
additional_information: Progression to supervisory positions is possible with additional training or experience.

units.title: Janitors, caretakers and heavy-duty cleaners
units.sub_major_group_title: Support occupations in cleaning and related services
units.definition: Janitors, caretakers and heavy-duty cleaners clean and maintain the interior and exterior of commercial, institutional and residential buildings and their surrounding grounds. They are employed by office and apartment building management companies, condominium corporations, educational institutions, health care facilities, recreational and shopping facilities, religious, industrial and other establishments.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Janitors, caretakers and heavy-duty cleaners clean and maintain the interior and exterior of commercial, institutional and residential buildings and their surrounding grounds.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school may be required.
Previous cleaning experience may be required.
additional_information: Progression to supervisory positions is possible with additional training or experience.

core_competencies.statement: to diagnose and resolve issues with cleaning equipment or unexpected problems that arise during maintenance

core_competencies.competency: Operation Monitoring of Machinery and Equipment
core_competencies.statement: to oversee the use of tools and equipment, perform regular checks to prevent malfunctions and ensure optimal performance
to handle and move heavy equipment or furniture, such as industrial vacuums and floor scrubbers
to perform tasks that require bending, crouching and stretching in various positions in order to access all areas that need cleaning or maintenance
to ensure all areas are thoroughly cleaned and maintained, including hard-to-reach spots
to be familiar with the chemical properties of cleaning agents and use products safely, according to manufacturer guidelines

units.title: Dry cleaning, laundry and related occupations
units.sub_major_group_title: Support occupations in cleaning and related services
units.definition: Dry cleaning and laundry machine operators operate machines to dry-clean or launder garments and other articles. Dry cleaning and laundry inspectors and assemblers check finished garments and other articles to ensure that they meet required standards for dry-cleaning, laundering and pressing, and assemble and bag finished garments and other articles. This unit group also includes workers who iron, press or otherwise finish garments and household articles. They are employed in dry cleaning, laundry and fur cleaning establishments, and in the laundries of hotels, hospitals and other institutions.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

oasis_profiles.profile_title: Dry cleaning and laundry machine operators
oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Dry cleaning, laundry and related occupations
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Dry cleaning and laundry machine operators operate machines to dry-clean and launder garments and other articles.
main_duties: Operate dry cleaning machines to dry-clean dresses, suits, coats, sweaters and other garments, draperies, cushion covers and other articles
Sort garments and operate washing machines and dryers to clean and dry clothing, sheets, blankets, towels and other articles
Operate machines to clean and blow-dry fur garments
Operate machines to dry-clean, dye, spray, re-oil, and re-buff suede and leather garments
Mix and add detergents, dyes, bleaches, starches and other solutions and chemicals.
employment_requirements: Some secondary school education is required.
Suede, leather and fur cleaners may require experience as dry cleaning or laundry machine operators.
Dry cleaning and laundry machine operators may require experience as inspectors or assemblers.
Four to eight weeks of on-the-job training are usually provided.
additional_information: Progression to supervisory dry cleaning and laundry occupations is possible with additional training and experience.
Workers in this group may specialize in finishing suede, leather, fur and delicate articles.
workplaces_employers: Laundries of hotels, hospitals and other institutions

oasis_profiles.profile_title: Dry cleaning and laundry inspectors and assemblers
oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Dry cleaning, laundry and related occupations
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Dry cleaning and laundry inspectors and assemblers check finished garments and other articles to make sure that they meet required standards for dry cleaning, laundering and pressing, and assemble and bag finished garments.
main_duties: Check finished garments and other articles to ensure that they are properly dry-cleaned or laundered
Record damaged or improperly dry-cleaned or laundered garments and other articles
Use hand-held steam iron to touch up finished garments and other articles
Assemble and bag finished garments and other articles
Match invoices with tags on garments or other articles.
employment_requirements: Some secondary school education is required.
Four to eight weeks of on-the-job training are usually provided.
additional_information: Progression to supervisory dry cleaning and laundry occupations is possible with additional training and experience.
Workers in this group may specialize in finishing suede, leather, fur and delicate articles.
workplaces_employers: Laundries of hotels, hospitals and other institutions

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Dry cleaning, laundry and related occupations
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Ironing, pressing and finishing workers iron, press or otherwise finish garments and household articles.
main_duties: Operate finishing equipment such as steam pressers, and use hand irons to finish pants, jackets, shirts, skirts and other dry cleaned and laundered articles
Hand iron laundered fine linens or dry-cleaned silk garments
Operate fur ironing and glazing equipment to finish fur garments and other fur articles
Fold and bag dry cleaned and laundered articles
May operate laundering equipment.
employment_requirements: Some secondary school education is required.
Four to eight weeks of on-the-job training are usually provided.
additional_information: Progression to supervisory dry cleaning and laundry occupations is possible with additional training and experience.
Workers in this group may specialize in finishing suede, leather, fur and delicate articles.
workplaces_employers: Laundries of hotels, hospitals and other institutions

units.title: Other service support occupations
units.sub_major_group_title: Support occupations in cleaning and related services
units.definition: Workers in other service support occupations perform a range of services. They are employed in a wide variety of establishments: places of employment are usually indicated in the job title.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Beauty salon attendants provide services in beauty salons.
main_duties: Shampoo, condition and dry customers' hair and assist hair stylists as directed
Keep work areas clean.
employment_requirements: There are no specific education requirements for occupations in this unit group.

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Door attendants provide services in retail service establishments.
main_duties: Assist persons entering or leaving residential buildings, theatres and similar establishments
May call taxis and assist with parcels.
employment_requirements: There are no specific education requirements for occupations in this unit group.

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Funeral home attendants provide services for funeral homes.
main_duties: Drive hearses
Arrange lights and floral displays
Escort mourners and act as pallbearers
Clean funeral parlours and chapels.
employment_requirements: There are no specific education requirements for occupations in this unit group.
A valid driver's licence is required for some occupations in this unit group, such as funeral attendant and car jockey.

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Laundromat attendants provide services in laundromats.
main_duties: Replenish vending machines
Provide change
Explain operation of machines to customers
Clean the laundromat and arrange for the repair of broken machines
May wash, dry and fold laundry for customers
May operate dry cleaning machines for customers.
employment_requirements: There are no specific education requirements for occupations in this unit group.

oasis_profiles.profile_title: Parking lot attendants and car jockeys
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Parking lot attendants and car jockeys provide services in parking lots.
main_duties: Collect parking fees and issue ticket stubs
Direct customers to parking spaces and park cars.
employment_requirements: There are no specific education requirements for occupations in this unit group.
A valid driver's licence is required for some occupations in this unit group, such as funeral attendant and car jockey.

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Ticket takers and ushers provide services in retail service establishments.
main_duties: Collect admission tickets or passes from patrons at entertainment events and direct patrons to their seats.
employment_requirements: There are no specific education requirements for occupations in this unit group.

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Other elemental workers perform services specific to the establishments in which their occupations are found.
main_duties: Perform services specific to the establishments in which their occupations are found.
employment_requirements: There are no specific education requirements for occupations in this unit group.

Drone pilot with a maximum takeoff drone weight of 25 kilograms must pass an online exam (small basic exam) and for takeoff weight of above 25 kilograms, they must pass an online exam (small advanced exam) and an in-person flight review with Transport Canada to get their Drone pilot certificate – advanced operations.
